CH 01 — SIGNAL OPEN

MEGALITH

Persistent broadcast infrastructure for the post-platform era.

Built by Reverie Collective

CH 02 — THE PROBLEM

Creators don't own
distribution

  • Streams end — presence disappears
  • Platforms censor, throttle, remove access overnight
  • Content is fragmented across a dozen services
  • Nothing persists. Nothing you create is yours.
CH 03 — THE SHIFT

Transmit on your own domain.
Claim your territory.

Before
  • Posts
  • Moments
  • Rented platforms
Now
  • Channels
  • Continuous presence
  • Owned systems
CH 04 — THE SOCIAL PROBLEM

Connected,
but out of sync

  • Social media is saturated — posts, feeds, short loops on repeat
  • Interaction is shallow. Communities are fragmented.
  • The algorithm optimises for engagement, not presence
  • There is no shared time. No ambient togetherness.
CH 05 — A NEW MODE

Ambient
presence

You don't log on.
You tune in.

Shared time. Continuous signal. Something always on.

CH 06 — WHAT MEGALITH IS

Maintain your
own signal

  • 24/7 continuous channels — no offline state
  • Upload → schedule → stream. Done.
  • Not a platform. Infrastructure.
  • Run your own signal.
CH 07 — HOW IT WORKS

Simple
by design

01
Upload
Add video to your channel vault
02
Schedule
Loop, queue, or time-block your programming
03
Broadcast
Channel runs continuously — always on air
04
Tune in
Drop in anytime. Something's always happening.

CH 09 — WHAT IT FEELS LIKE

Interplanetary
Public Access

  • Public access TV — but you own the tower
  • Ambient broadcast — drop in anytime, something's always on
  • Drop in any time. Something is always transmitting.
  • No curator. No gatekeeper. Just the signal.
CH 10 — WHO IT'S FOR

Chaos engine
for the Raconteurs

A
Artists
24/7 channel as practice, archive, and living presence
B
Brands
Owned broadcast layer. No algorithm tax, no rent.
C
Collectives
Community systems with shared, programmable broadcast
D
Institutions
Galleries, labels, publishers — always-on ambient presence
CH 11 — OPTIONAL LAYERS

Customize
your signal

  • Token gating — hold a key, access the signal
  • NFT-based time-slot systems — sell your broadcast window
  • Private and hidden chat layers
  • Custom permissions per channel
  • All optional. All composable. Nothing forced.
CH 12 — BUSINESS MODEL
Channel deploy
Setup and configuration for select channels and brands on the primary Megalith network
Infrastructure
Ongoing hosting, support, and broadcast pipeline
White-label
Custom deployments for media companies and networks
Token systems
Optional NFT and key integrations for channel monetisation
NOT AD-BASED. NOT ALGORITHM-BASED.
CH 13 — WHY NOW

Activation,
not invention

  • Creators are leaving platforms — trust is broken at the root
  • Streaming dominates culture but no one really owns their creations
  • No tools exist for persistent, creator-controlled channels
  • This system is built, tested, and functional today
CH 14 — PROOF OF SIGNAL

Scalable
cultural infrastructure

✓
Codebase
Existing, functional repo — not a prototype
✓
Real users
Active user base on MGLTH.TV
✓
Live system
Continuous streaming pipeline in production
✓
Multi-service
Broadcast infrastructure across multiple services
CH 15 — VISION

From isolated creators
→ cultural infrastructure

A network of independent channels. Always-on signals. Creator-controlled media systems.

The internet has social graphs.
It doesn't have a broadcast layer. Yet.

CH 16 — HOW THIS WORKS

The network
is not for sale

THE NETWORK
Curated by invitation.
  • First-round investors earn a channel
  • Not because they paid
  • Because they belong there
THE INFRASTRUCTURE
That part is for sale.
  • Solo — single upload seat
  • Collective — multi-contributor permissions
  • Enterprise — full white-label deployment
If you're reading this, you already know which one you are.
